What changed is the Codex execution environment, not your Entire login.

Evidence from this task:

- Entire is present at `/Users/thomi/.local/bin/entire`, version `0.8.42`.
- The sandbox can read the `ashtom` login context but cannot access its host credential, so it reports “Not logged in.”
- Running outside the sandbox should solve that, but Codex’s approval service fails before execution with an internal HTTP 400:

- The command never reaches Entire.

This conversation is using a restricted, network-blocked sandbox with auto-reviewed escalations. The prior conversations likely had host access, a previously approved `entire` command prefix, or a functioning approval reviewer. I can’t identify which without their task metadata, but the immediate blocker is a Codex approval-routing regression/configuration mismatch—not authentication or trail permissions.
I moved to Full access. Try again now.

Full access removes the credential and network boundary that blocked the command. I’m retrying the authenticated trail read now, then I’ll inspect the linked work and report only actionable adversarial findings.

content.text:
I would block trail 10 at commit `617fc5e`. CI and `go test -race ./...` pass, but the ranking safeguards are incomplete.

### Findings

1. **High — zero-evidence neighbors still receive the full caller boost.**  
   The conservative path uses `0.28 × seed`, but then unconditionally adds up to `+8` caller boost. A term-free neighbor of a 15-point seed can score about `13.96`, displacing direct lexical matches and contradicting the trail’s explicit safety claim. [Code](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L1930-L1938)

2. **Medium — deep sparse search bypasses the body-only half-boost.**  
   Sparse candidates carry only `sparse-region`; `searchBodyOnlyEvidence` recognizes only `body`. Consequently, `--top-k > 20` gives incidental body matches the full caller boost before sparse ranking. [Evidence check](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L1829-L1839), [sparse signal](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L1373-L1389)

3. **Medium — common test layouts are counted as production callers.**  
   The matcher misses `spec/`, `*_test.py`, `*_spec.rb`, and directories such as `Project.Tests/`. Those callers now vote in caller-degree scoring and also evade the stronger test penalty. The new test covers only `test/` and `_test.go`. [Caller exclusion](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L1765-L1778), [path matcher](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L3127-L3131)

4. **Medium — `fast` emits relations its documented contract says it excludes.**  
   Shallow filtering applies only to `CALLS`, not `CONSTRUCTS`. After this commit enables the global short-name index, `fast` emits `name_only` constructors despite claiming name-only resolution remains full-only. Running the branch binary against `entire-brain` produced 16 such records at confidence `0.68`. [Filter](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/provider.go#L952-L959), [index expansion](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/provider.go#L2006-L2012)

5. **Low — `caller_boosted_candidates` under-reports deep-search behavior.**  
   The first boost count is stored, but the later sparse-candidate boost discards its return value. Results can therefore carry `graph:callers` while the statistic reports zero or too few candidates. [Code](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/blob/617fc5e6336f4d4fd8594753a44f8f581c83f8eb/internal/sem/search.go#L496-L545)

There is also a review-state gap: the identical earlier PR #52 already reported findings 1 and 2, but trail 10 currently reports zero findings and its replacement PR has no reviews. No files or trail state were changed.
Document them on the trail and address them

I’ll record each validated issue as a finding on trail 10, switch to its branch, implement the ranking/profile fixes with regression tests, run the race suite, then resolve the findings against the new commit. I’ll preserve any unrelated local changes if the worktree isn’t clean.

plan.step: Inspect worktree and trail-finding command schema
Add five findings to trail 10
Implement fixes and regression tests on the trail branch
Run formatting, vet, race tests, and targeted behavior checks
Commit/push changes and resolve trail findings with evidence

result.output: Plan updated

input.input:
const results = await Promise.all([
  tools.exec_command({"cmd":"entire trail finding add --trail 10 --severity high --confidence 0.99 --file internal/sem/search.go --line 1937 --client-id codex-trail10-zero-evidence-hub-617fc5e --body 'Zero-evidence graph neighbors still receive the full caller-degree boost. The conservative 0.28*seed path unconditionally adds up to 8 points from callerBoosts, allowing an unrelated hub to rank just below a strong seed. Gate or attenuate caller boost when the neighbor has no query evidence and add a regression test.' --instruction 'Do not add the full caller-degree boost to graph-expanded neighbors without query evidence.' --json","workdir":"/Users/thomi/Projects/entire-graph","yield_time_ms":30000,"max_output_tokens":20000}),
  tools.exec_command({"cmd":"entire trail finding add --trail 10 --severity medium --confidence 0.99 --file internal/sem/search.go --line 1839 --client-id codex-trail10-sparse-body-boost-617fc5e --body 'Deep sparse candidates carry only the sparse-region signal, while searchBodyOnlyEvidence recognizes only body. content.text: All five findings are now open on trail 10 with stable idempotency keys. The trail’s line anchoring selected stale nearby text for three locations, but the bodies and file targets are correct; I’ll update or resolve them with the fixing commit so the final evidence points at the new code version.

@@
-		// shallow call resolution, keep only high-precision calls; drop

+	// type-like symbols keeps this high-precision while recovering cross-file
+	// same-package calls and constructions that the globally-unique gate below
+	// would otherwise drop.
*** End Patch
